heroHeadline: Iran Seizes Two Ships in Hormuz Hours After Ceasefire Extended; Talks in Limbo on Day 54
heroSubtitle: Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely on April 21 (at Pakistan's request), averting a resumption of strikes — but the US naval blockade remains fully in force. Hours later on April 22, Iran's IRGC seized two container ships (MSC Francesca and Epaminondas) in the Strait of Hormuz and fired upon a third, framing the seizures as enforcement of Iran's Hormuz transit rights. Talks remain in limbo with Iran's FM Araghchi refusing to negotiate 'under the shadow of threats.' Separately, Trump's Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh vowed at his April 21 Senate hearing he would not be Trump's 'sock puppet,' and a new Verasight poll put Trump's approval at a second-term low of 35%, with prices/inflation net approval at -46.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF.
